title: Macau 14-Day City Break
metaTitle: Macau 14-Day City Break | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Macau, Macau SAR, China: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy and House of Dancing Water show. Prices from $2536 per person (estimate).
highlights: Suite stay and private gaming room at a Cotai Strip resort
Hong Kong via high-speed ferry (1 hour)
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Macau, Macau SAR, China — Subtropical climate with hot, humid summers (June-August, frequent typhoon risk) and mild, dry winters (December-February, highs around 18-20°C). Spring and autumn are transitional and generally pleasant. October to December offers the most comfortable temperatures, low humidity, and clear skies — ideal for walking the historic center and coinciding with the Macau Grand Prix in November. This itinerary blends Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy, House of Dancing Water show, Ruins of St. Paul's with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy, House of Dancing Water show, Ruins of St. Paul's
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Macau
itinerary.description: Land at Macau International Airport (MFM) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy — Glass-floor observation deck and the world's highest commercial bungee jump. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Macau: House of Dancing Water show & The Venetian Macao gondola ride
itinerary.description: Morning visit to House of Dancing Water show — Spectacular water-stage theatrical production at City of Dreams. In the afternoon, continue to The Venetian Macao gondola ride — Indoor canal ride through a recreated Venice under a painted sky. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Macau: Grand Lisboa observation & casino tour & Macau Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Grand Lisboa observation & casino tour — Iconic lotus-shaped skyscraper, Macau's casino skyline landmark. In the afternoon, continue to Macau Museum — History of Macau's 400+ years as a Portuguese trading post. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Hong Kong via high-speed ferry (1 hour)
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Hong Kong via high-speed ferry (1 hour), a popular side trip from Macau. Return to Macau in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Hong Kong via high-speed ferry (1 hour)

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Coloane Village's quiet fishing-town lanes — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Macau: Coloane Village bike & beach day & Ruins of St. Paul's
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Coloane Village bike & beach day — Rent a bike to explore Macau's quieter, greener southern island. In the afternoon, continue to Ruins of St. Paul's — Iconic 17th-century cathedral façade, Macau's most photographed landmark. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Zhuhai, mainland China, via the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Zhuhai, mainland China, via the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge, a popular side trip from Macau. Return to Macau in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Zhuhai, mainland China, via the Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge

itinerary.title: Macau: Senado Square & A-Ma Temple
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Senado Square — UNESCO-listed plaza with wave-patterned Portuguese mosaic paving. In the afternoon, continue to A-Ma Temple — Macau's oldest temple, dating to 1488, dedicated to the sea goddess Mazu. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Mount Fortress gardens at sunset — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Taipa and Coloane island villages
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Taipa and Coloane island villages, a popular side trip from Macau. Return to Macau in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Taipa and Coloane island villages

itinerary.title: Macau: Fortaleza do Monte & Rua de São Paulo
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Fortaleza do Monte — Historic 17th-century fortress with panoramic city views. In the afternoon, continue to Rua de São Paulo — Cobblestone street of colonial shophouses leading to the Ruins. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Macau: Taipa Houses-Museum grounds & Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Taipa Houses-Museum grounds — Pastel-colored Portuguese colonial residences by the water. In the afternoon, continue to Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy — Glass-floor observation deck and the world's highest commercial bungee jump. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Lou Lim Ieoc Garden — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to House of Dancing Water show or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to MFM for your departure flight.

travelTips: Casino resorts on the Cotai Strip run free shuttle buses to/from the ferry terminal and border gate — no taxi needed.
Bring your passport even for short casino visits; ID checks are routine.
The historic center is best explored on foot in the morning before Senado Square gets crowded with day-trippers from Hong Kong.
MOP and HKD are both widely accepted at a roughly 1:1 rate, though change is usually given in MOP.
Book House of Dancing Water tickets in advance — shows regularly sell out on weekends.
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Light layers — malls and casinos are heavily air-conditioned
Umbrella during the June-September wet season
thingsToDo: SkyWalk X around the tower rim
thingsToAvoid: Photographing the gaming floor — casino security will intervene immediately.
Underdressed casual wear (flip-flops, swimwear) at premium gaming areas and fine-dining restaurants.
Assuming Hong Kong dollars and Macau patacas are interchangeable everywhere outside casinos and tourist areas.
Visiting Senado Square at peak midday on weekends, when day-trip ferry crowds from Hong Kong are heaviest.
topRestaurants: Robuchon au Dôme — French Fine Dining (3 Michelin Stars) ($$$$)
Lord Stow's Bakery — Portuguese Egg Tarts ($)
Wong Chi Kei Congee & Noodle — Cantonese Street Food ($)
shoppingAreas: Rua de São Paulo souvenir shops
nightlife: Casino floors of The Venetian and Grand Lisboa
Rooftop bars overlooking the Macau skyline
hiddenGems: Old Taipa backstreet egg tart bakeries away from the crowds
adventureActivities: SkyWalk X around the tower rim
luxuryExperiences: Suite stay and private gaming room at a Cotai Strip resort
Michelin-starred tasting menu at Robuchon au Dôme
Private junk boat cruise around the Macau peninsula
VIP House of Dancing Water seating
honeymoonExperiences: Sunset stroll through Senado Square and the Ruins of St. Paul's
Romantic gondola ride at The Venetian
Couples spa at a Cotai Strip resort
Rooftop dinner overlooking the illuminated skyline
budgetExperiences: Free walking tour of the UNESCO historic center
Egg tarts and street food crawl for a few dollars a stop
Casino floor people-watching (no minimum to enter)
Free ferry-view of the skyline from the Macau peninsula waterfront

festivalCalendar.name: Macau Grand Prix (street circuit motor race)
A-Ma Temple Festival
festivalCalendar.timing: April/May (23rd day of the 3rd lunar month)

festivalCalendar.name: Feast of the Drunken Dragon
festivalCalendar.timing: May (8th day of the 4th lunar month)

localEtiquette: Casinos enforce a strict minimum age of 21 and formal-casual dress in premium gaming rooms — flip-flops and tank tops are often turned away.
Photography is prohibited on the gaming floor of any casino.
Tipping isn't mandatory but a 10% service charge is common at restaurants; rounding up taxi fares is appreciated.
The historic center blends Portuguese and Chinese culture — locals take pride in both heritages, so avoid treating it as "just a casino town."
Business cards are exchanged with both hands, a habit carried over from Chinese and Portuguese trading customs.
localTransportGuide: Macau is compact and highly walkable in the historic center; free shuttle buses run from casino resorts to the ferry terminal and border gate. Public buses cover the rest of the peninsula cheaply, and taxis are metered and reasonably priced. The Hong Kong-Zhuhai-Macau Bridge and regular ferries connect easily to Hong Kong.
bestTimeToVisit: October to December offers the most comfortable temperatures, low humidity, and clear skies — ideal for walking the historic center and coinciding with the Macau Grand Prix in November.
weather: Subtropical climate with hot, humid summers (June-August, frequent typhoon risk) and mild, dry winters (December-February, highs around 18-20°C). Spring and autumn are transitional and generally pleasant.
visaInformation: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can enter visa-free for 30-90 days depending on nationality. Many nationalities can also visit as part of a Hong Kong side-trip via ferry. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.
averageFlightTime: ~10.4h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Macau?
faqs.answer: Most Western nationalities including the US, UK, Canada, Australia and EU citizens can enter visa-free for 30-90 days depending on nationality. Many nationalities can also visit as part of a Hong Kong side-trip via ferry. Verify current requirements against your specific passport before travel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Macau?
faqs.answer: October to December offers the most comfortable temperatures, low humidity, and clear skies — ideal for walking the historic center and coinciding with the Macau Grand Prix in November. Subtropical climate with hot, humid summers (June-August, frequent typhoon risk) and mild, dry winters (December-February, highs around 18-20°C). Spring and autumn are transitional and generally pleasant.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Macau safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Macau is rated very safe for tourists, with low rates of violent crime. Usual travel precautions (watch belongings in crowds, use licensed taxis) still apply.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Macau?
faqs.answer: The local currency is MOP. Cards and mobile payments are widely accepted, and ATMs are easy to find.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: Sofia Brown
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Booked this as a solo trip and it was well paced. Macau Museum in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Olivia Garcia
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Our trip to Macau was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Ruins of St. Paul's was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Ethan Fernandez
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Macau Museum and Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Liam Williams
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: Our trip to Macau was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Senado Square was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Lukas Wang
reviews.title: Perfect for our honeymoon
reviews.body: Macau is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Macau Tower Skywalk & Bungy. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Priya Garcia
reviews.title: Would book again in a heartbeat
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Fortaleza do Monte in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Tanaka
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Ruins of St. Paul's alone was worth the trip, but combined with A-Ma Temple this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Omar Ibrahim
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Taipa Houses-Museum grounds and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.

reviews.author: Emma Kowalski
